questions.question: At the pI of glycine, the molecule exists predominantly as

answers.d: A neutral molecule with no charge

questions.question: What does the pI (isoelectric point) of glycine represent?

answers.a: The pH at which glycine is most soluble
answers.b: The pH at which glycine has no net electrical charge
answers.c: The pH at which glycine is completely ionized
answers.d: The pH at which glycine is most reactive

questions.question: Why is glycine considered an amphoteric molecule?

answers.a: It contains both acidic and basic functional groups.
answers.c: It is soluble in both acidic and basic solutions.
answers.d: It remains neutral at all pH values.

questions.question: Which two groups in glycine are involved in the protonation and deprotonation processes during titration?
